description: Frontend Redesign — Bold & playful branded design system, collapsible sidebar, command palette, gallery views, drag-and-drop, animations, smart empty states, and full component restructure

userStories.title: Install new dependencies for redesign
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all new packages installed so that subsequent stories can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: Install framer-motion for animations
Install @dnd-kit/core @dnd-kit/sortable @dnd-kit/utilities for drag-and-drop
Install cmdk for command palette
Install sonner for toast notifications
Install @radix-ui/react-tooltip @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu @radix-ui/react-select @radix-ui/react-tabs @radix-ui/react-scroll-area as Radix primitives
Install @fontsource/nunito and @fontsource/inter for branded fonts
All packages install without errors
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create branded color tokens and typography in index.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I want branded design tokens so that all components use the GeekVault navy/gold palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace grayscale OKLch tokens in src/web/src/index.css with branded palette: primary deep navy (#1B3A6B), accent gold (#E8A838), background warm white (#FAFAF8), card white, muted blue-gray, destructive red, success green
Dark mode tokens: background deep navy-black (#0D1B2A), card dark (#142237), muted navy (#1E3250), primary lighter navy (#3B6CB5), accent gold same
Sidebar tokens: dark navy gradient background in BOTH light and dark themes
Import @fontsource/nunito and @fontsource/inter in index.css
Set font-family: Nunito for headings via a .font-display utility, Inter for body text as default
Define spacing scale tokens: --space-1 (0.25rem) through --space-12 (3rem)
Define radius tokens: --radius-sm (0.375rem), --radius-md (0.625rem), --radius-lg (1rem), --radius-full (9999px)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create design system Card and Badge components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want branded Card and Badge components so that pages use consistent visual building blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/card.tsx: Card component with subtle shadow, hover lift animation (transform + shadow transition), optional accent border-top (gold variant), supports className prop
Create src/web/src/components/ds/badge.tsx: Badge with variants default, primary, accent, success, warning, destructive, outline; sizes sm and md
Create src/web/src/components/ds/index.ts barrel export for all DS components
Both components respect light/dark theme via CSS variables
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create design system EmptyState and PageHeader components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reusable EmptyState and PageHeader components for consistent page structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/empty-state.tsx: Centered layout with icon slot (ReactNode), title, description, action button (optional onClick + label), generous padding (py-16), brand colors
Create src/web/src/components/ds/page-header.tsx: Page title (large, font-display), optional description, optional right-side actions slot (ReactNode), consistent across all pages
Export both from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create design system Skeleton and StatCard components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Skeleton loaders and StatCard components for dashboard and loading states.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/skeleton.tsx: Skeleton primitives — SkeletonRect, SkeletonCircle, SkeletonText (multiple lines) with pulse animation, configurable width/height
Create src/web/src/components/ds/stat-card.tsx: StatCard with icon (ReactNode), label, value, optional trend (up/down/neutral with color), uses branded Card underneath
Export both from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create design system Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Select, Textarea, Tabs, and ScrollArea
userStories.description: As a developer, I want styled Radix-based primitives to replace native HTML elements throughout the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/tooltip.tsx wrapping @radix-ui/react-tooltip with branded styling (dark bg, white text, rounded)
Create src/web/src/components/ds/dropdown-menu.tsx wrapping @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu with branded styling (card bg, shadow, rounded items with hover state)
Create src/web/src/components/ds/select.tsx wrapping @radix-ui/react-select with branded styling (border, rounded, focus ring in gold accent)
Create src/web/src/components/ds/textarea.tsx matching the existing input.tsx design patterns
Create src/web/src/components/ds/tabs.tsx wrapping @radix-ui/react-tabs with branded styling (underline active tab in gold accent)
Create src/web/src/components/ds/scroll-area.tsx wrapping @radix-ui/react-scroll-area
Export all from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create design system DataTable component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable DataTable component for sortable, filterable tables with loading and empty states.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/data-table.tsx with generic typed API: DataTable<T>
Props: columns (header, accessor, sortable flag, render function), data array, onSort callback, loading boolean, emptyState ReactNode
Column headers are clickable when sortable — show sort direction arrow indicator
Loading state renders skeleton rows (use Skeleton component)
Empty state renders provided emptyState node when data is empty
Responsive: horizontal scroll wrapper on mobile
Row hover highlighting
Export from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create animation utilities with framer-motion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared animation wrappers so that transitions feel consistent across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/motion.tsx with: PageTransition (fade-in + slide-up wrapper), FadeIn (configurable delay), StaggerChildren (parent that staggers child animations for lists/grids)
Create src/web/src/components/ds/animated-number.tsx: Smoothly animates number value changes using framer-motion's animate/useSpring
All motion components respect prefers-reduced-motion media query (skip animations if set)
Export from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create toast notification system with Sonner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a toast system for non-intrusive success/error feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/toaster.tsx wrapping Sonner's Toaster with branded styling (match brand colors)
Add Toaster component to main.tsx (rendered once at app root)
Toast variants available: success (green), error (red), info (blue), warning (gold)
Toasts appear at bottom-right, auto-dismiss after 4 seconds
Export a simple toast utility: toast.success(msg), toast.error(msg), toast.info(msg), toast.warning(msg)
Export from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create drag-and-drop SortableList component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable SortableList component for drag-and-drop reordering.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/ds/sortable-list.tsx using @dnd-kit/core and @dnd-kit/sortable
Generic typed API: SortableList<T> with items array, renderItem function, onReorder(newOrder) callback, keyExtractor function
Supports both vertical list and grid layout modes
Dragged item shows shadow + slight scale effect, drop target area highlighted
Keyboard accessible (built into dnd-kit)
Touch-friendly for mobile
Export from ds/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add logo assets and favicon
userStories.description: As a user, I want the GeekVault brand visible in the browser tab and throughout the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/assets/ directory
Copy the logo image as src/web/src/assets/logo-full.png (full logo with text)
Create a cropped/extracted vault icon version as src/web/src/assets/vault-icon.png (just the vault, no text)
Generate favicon files: replace src/web/public/vite.svg with vault-icon based favicon.ico or favicon.svg
Update src/web/index.html: set favicon to vault icon, set page title to GeekVault
Add apple-touch-icon link in index.html head
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use the ChatGPT Image Mar 14, 2026, 07_10_32 PM.png file from the project root for the logo. The vault is on the left side of the image and the text GeekVault is on the right.

userStories.title: Restructure frontend into feature-based folder architecture
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend organized into features/ and components/layout/ folders for maintainability.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory structure: src/features/auth/, src/features/dashboard/, src/features/collections/, src/features/collection-types/, src/features/wishlist/, src/features/profile/
Create src/components/layout/ directory
Move src/pages/Login.tsx to src/features/auth/login-page.tsx (update component name if needed)
Move src/pages/Register.tsx to src/features/auth/register-page.tsx
Move src/pages/Dashboard.tsx to src/features/dashboard/dashboard-page.tsx
Move src/pages/Collections.tsx to src/features/collections/collections-page.tsx
Move src/pages/CollectionDetail.tsx to src/features/collections/collection-detail-page.tsx
Move src/pages/CatalogItemDetail.tsx to src/features/collections/catalog-item-detail-page.tsx
Move src/pages/CollectionTypes.tsx to src/features/collection-types/collection-types-page.tsx
Move src/pages/Wishlist.tsx to src/features/wishlist/wishlist-page.tsx
Move src/pages/Profile.tsx to src/features/profile/profile-page.tsx
Move src/components/app-layout.tsx to src/components/layout/app-layout.tsx
Update all imports in App.tsx to use new paths
Delete empty src/pages/ directory
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep the page components as-is for now — just move files. Decomposition into sub-components happens in later stories.

userStories.title: Create shared API client and hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a centralized API client and shared hooks so that pages don't duplicate fetch logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/lib/api.ts with: a fetchApi helper that injects auth token from localStorage, sets Content-Type, handles base URL, parses JSON responses, throws on non-OK status
Create src/web/src/hooks/use-debounce.ts: useDebounce<T>(value, delay) hook
Create src/web/src/hooks/use-media-query.ts: useMediaQuery(query) hook returning boolean
Export hooks from src/web/src/hooks/index.ts barrel
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redesign sidebar as collapsible with brand styling
userStories.description: As a user, I want a collapsible sidebar that I can shrink to icon-only mode for more screen space.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/layout/sidebar.tsx extracted from app-layout.tsx
Desktop sidebar has two states: expanded (~256px with logo+labels) and collapsed (~64px with icons only)
Toggle button (chevron icon) at bottom of sidebar switches between states
Sidebar state persists to localStorage key 'geekvault-sidebar-collapsed'
Default state on first visit: expanded on desktop (>=1024px), collapsed on tablet (768px-1023px)
Smooth width transition animation (200-300ms ease)
Collapsed state shows Tooltip on hover for each nav item (using DS Tooltip component)
Sidebar background: dark navy gradient in both light and dark themes (use sidebar CSS tokens from US-002)
Sidebar text/icons: white/light in both themes
Active nav item: gold accent left border + subtle background tint
Logo: show full logo when expanded, vault icon only when collapsed
User section at bottom: avatar + display name (hidden when collapsed) + logout button
Mobile: keep sheet/drawer behavior (hamburger opens full overlay sidebar, always expanded)
Update app-layout.tsx to use new Sidebar component
Create src/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx for the mobile top header bar
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Nav items: Dashboard (LayoutDashboard), Collections (Library), Collection Types (Layers), Wishlist (Heart), Profile (User)

userStories.title: Add command palette with Cmd+K
userStories.description: As a user, I want to press Cmd+K to quickly search and navigate across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/web/src/components/layout/command-palette.tsx using cmdk package
Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) opens the command palette overlay
Search input at top with i18n placeholder text
Results grouped into sections: Navigation (Dashboard, Collections, Collection Types, Wishlist, Profile), Actions (Create Collection, Toggle Theme, Toggle Sidebar, Change Language)
Each result shows: icon + title
Enter navigates to selected result or executes action
Arrow keys navigate, Escape closes
Empty state shows 'No results found' message
Render CommandPalette in app-layout.tsx
All text uses i18n translation keys (add commandPalette.* keys to en.json and pt.json)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: For MVP, just do navigation + static actions. Collection/item search can be added later.

userStories.title: Redesign login page with split-screen brand layout
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a visually appealing login page that introduces the GeekVault brand.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop split-screen: left panel (60%) with deep navy gradient background, large centered GeekVault logo, tagline text; right panel (40%) with form card
Form card: 'Welcome back' heading, email input, password input, submit button (primary blue), link to register
Inputs use branded focus ring (gold/accent color)
Submit button shows loading spinner during API call
Error messages shown below form in destructive color
Mobile: compact brand header with logo above form (stacked vertically)
Add i18n keys for tagline (auth.tagline), heading (auth.welcomeBack), etc. to en.json and pt.json
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign register page with split-screen brand layout
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a visually appealing register page matching the login design.
acceptanceCriteria: Same split-screen layout as login page (reuse the brand panel component)
Form card: 'Create your vault' heading, display name input, email input, password input, submit button, link to login
Same branded input styling as login
Submit button shows loading spinner during API call
Inline form validation with error messages
Mobile: same stacked layout as login
Add i18n keys for heading (auth.createYourVault) to en.json and pt.json
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extract shared AuthLayout or BrandPanel component from login page to reuse here.

userStories.title: Redesign dashboard page with branded stats and skeleton loaders
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished dashboard with animated stats, branded charts, and skeleton loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PageHeader: 'Dashboard' title with welcome message 'Welcome back, {displayName}'
Stats row using StatCard components: Total Collections (Library icon), Total Items (Package icon), Owned Copies (Box icon), Estimated Value (DollarSign icon), Total Invested (TrendingUp icon)
Stat values use AnimatedNumber for smooth transitions
Charts section (2-column on desktop, stacked mobile): Pie chart (items by condition) and Bar chart (condition breakdown) using brand colors (navy, gold, teal variations)
Charts wrapped in DS Card components with headers
Collection summaries section: grid of DS Cards with cover image placeholder, name, item count, total value
Recent acquisitions section: use DataTable with columns Thumbnail, Name, Collection, Condition (Badge), Date, Price
Full skeleton loading state (not just spinner) while data loads
Use StaggerChildren animation for card grids on load
Replace inline success/error messages with toast notifications
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Decompose into sub-components in src/features/dashboard/components/: stats-row.tsx, charts-section.tsx, recent-acquisitions.tsx, collection-summaries.tsx

userStories.title: Add smart empty states to dashboard and collections pages
userStories.description: As a new user, I want helpful empty states so I know what to do when pages have no data.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard empty state (no collections): Vault icon, 'Your vault is empty', 'Start by creating your first collection to track your collectibles', CTA button 'Create Collection' navigates to /collections?create=true

Collections page empty state (no collections): Library icon, 'No collections yet', 'Collections organize your items by type — comics, cards, figures, and more', CTA 'Create Your First Collection' opens create dialog
All empty state text uses i18n keys: add emptyStates.dashboard.*, emptyStates.collections.* to en.json and pt.json
Use the EmptyState DS component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign collections page with branded cards and search/filter
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished collections page with visual cards and filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PageHeader: 'Collections' + 'Create Collection' button (accent/gold style)

Collection cards redesigned using DS Card: cover image (aspect-ratio 16/9) or gradient fallback with icon, name (bold), type badge (DS Badge), item count, description (2 lines truncated), footer with 'View' button + overflow menu (DS DropdownMenu for Edit/Delete)
Hover: subtle lift + shadow animation on cards
Grid: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop
StaggerChildren animation on load
Search bar above grid: filter by collection name
Filter by type dropdown (DS Select)
Skeleton card placeholders while loading
Create/Edit dialog: use DS Select for type selector, image upload with visual dropzone (dashed border area)
Delete uses ConfirmDialog with destructive styling
Replace inline messages with toast notifications
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add empty states to collection detail, wishlist, and collection types pages
userStories.description: As a new user, I want empty states on all remaining pages with helpful CTAs.
acceptanceCriteria: Collection detail (no items): Package icon, 'No items in this collection', 'Add your first catalog item to start tracking what you own', CTA 'Add Item'
Wishlist (empty): Heart icon, 'Your wishlist is empty', 'Save items you are looking for and track target prices', CTA 'Add to Wishlist'
Collection types (none): Layers icon, 'No collection types defined', 'Collection types let you customize fields for different kinds of collectibles', CTA 'Create Collection Type'
All empty state text added as i18n keys in both en.json and pt.json under emptyStates.*
Use the EmptyState DS component
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign collection detail page toolbar and grid/table view toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between grid and table views for browsing items in a collection.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PageHeader: collection name + type badge + action buttons (Add Item, Export, Import)
Add view toggle buttons in toolbar area: Grid icon and List icon, one active at a time
View preference persists to localStorage per collection
Toolbar includes: search input, condition filter (DS Select), owned status filter, sort dropdown

Grid view: responsive image-first cards (2 col mobile, 3 col tablet, 4 col desktop) — each card shows primary image/placeholder, item name, identifier, owned count badge. Hover reveals quick action overlay
Table view: use DS DataTable with columns Thumbnail, Name, Identifier, Condition summary, Owned copies count, Estimated value. Sortable columns, row click navigates to detail
Both views show skeleton loaders while data loads
StaggerChildren animation on grid cards
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is a large page — focus on the toolbar + view toggle + basic grid/table rendering. Keep existing dialog/form logic intact, just restyle.

userStories.title: Redesign collection detail sets section
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sets section to use collapsible accordions with progress bars.
acceptanceCriteria: Sets displayed below items as collapsible accordion sections
Each set header: set name, completion percentage as a progress bar (gold fill on navy track), item count text (e.g. '5/12 items')
Click header to expand/collapse set items list
Set items: compact list with visual owned/unowned indicator (checkmark vs empty circle)
Create set and add items to set dialogs remain functional (restyle with branded components)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign collection detail import flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want a step-by-step import wizard with drag-and-drop file upload.
acceptanceCriteria: Import button opens a multi-step dialog/wizard
Step 1: Drag-and-drop file upload zone (dashed border, icon, 'Drop CSV file here or click to browse' text)
Step 2: Preview imported data in a table showing parsed rows with validation errors highlighted in red
Step 3: Confirm button to save, showing count of items to be imported
Progress/step indicator at top of wizard (Step 1 of 3, Step 2 of 3, etc.)
Back/Next/Confirm buttons for navigation between steps
Replace inline messages with toast notifications on success/error
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Redesign catalog item detail hero and info sections
userStories.description: As a user, I want a rich item detail page with a hero image area and clean field layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: large primary image (or placeholder) on left, item name (large heading) + identifier subtitle + collection breadcrumb on right
Breadcrumb navigation at top: Collections > {Collection Name} > {Item Name} (clickable links)
Action buttons: Edit, Delete, Add Owned Copy
Custom fields section: clean key-value grid layout with proper type rendering (dates formatted, booleans as indicators, enums as badges)
Description section with proper text formatting
Skeleton loader for initial page load
Replace inline messages with toast notifications

userStories.title: Redesign catalog item detail owned copies section
userStories.description: As a user, I want owned copies displayed as visual cards with condition badges.
acceptanceCriteria: Owned copies section: card-based layout (one DS Card per copy)
Each card: condition badge (colored, using DS Badge), purchase date, purchase price, estimated value, source, notes
Copy images shown as small thumbnail gallery within card
Per-copy actions: Edit and Delete buttons (Delete with confirmation dialog)
'Add Copy' button uses accent/gold styling
If no copies: show EmptyState component with Box icon and 'No copies owned yet' message

userStories.title: Redesign wishlist page with cards, grouping, and filters
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visually organized wishlist with card layout and filtering.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PageHeader: 'Wishlist' + 'Add Item' button
Items displayed as DS Cards: item name, collection badge, priority badge (high=red, medium=gold, low=gray using DS Badge), target price, notes preview (2 lines)
If linked to catalog item: show small thumbnail + link icon
Actions per card: Edit, Delete via DS DropdownMenu
Group items by collection with collapsible section headers
Filter/sort toolbar: filter by priority (All/High/Medium/Low via DS Select), sort by priority/price/date/name
StaggerChildren animation on cards
Skeleton loaders while fetching

userStories.title: Redesign collection types page with branded cards and field builder
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished collection types page with tag-style fields and drag-and-drop field builder.

Type cards redesigned with DS Card: large emoji icon at top, type name (bold), description (2 lines truncated), custom fields as compact tag/badge list (field name + type), action buttons (Edit, Delete with confirmation)
Grid layout: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop
Create/Edit dialog redesigned with DS Tabs: 'General' tab (name, description, icon) + 'Custom Fields' tab
Custom field builder: each field row has drag handle (using SortableList for reordering), field name input, type select (DS Select), required toggle, delete button
Enum type: inline tag editor (type text + Enter to add option, x to remove)
Field count indicator (e.g. '3/10 fields')

userStories.title: Redesign profile page with sections and toast feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean profile page with organized sections and non-blocking feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Use PageHeader: 'Profile'
Avatar section: large circular avatar (120px) with camera icon overlay on hover, click to upload, loading indicator during upload

Form organized in visual sections with dividers: Account Info (email disabled, display name), About (bio textarea using DS Textarea), Preferences (language DS Select, currency DS Select), Appearance (theme toggle as segmented control: light/dark/system with icons)
Save button: primary blue, sticky at bottom on mobile
Success/error feedback via toast notifications (remove inline messages)
Add i18n keys for section headers (profile.sections.*) to en.json and pt.json

userStories.title: Add page transitions to all routes
userStories.description: As a user, I want smooth transitions when navigating between pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap page content in PageTransition component (from DS motion.tsx) in App.tsx or app-layout.tsx
Each route change triggers a subtle fade-in + slide-up animation
Dialogs use scale-in animation (ScaleIn from motion.tsx or framer-motion AnimatePresence)
Respects prefers-reduced-motion: skip animations when set
No layout jump or flash during transitions

userStories.title: Add SortOrder column to CatalogItems and reorder API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SortOrder field on CatalogItems and a reorder endpoint so the frontend can persist drag-and-drop order.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SortOrder (int, default 0) property to CatalogItem entity in src/api/GeekVault.Api/Entities/Vault/CatalogItem.cs
Generate and apply EF Core migration: dotnet ef migrations add AddCatalogItemSortOrder
Update CatalogItemsRepository to order by SortOrder by default when returning items for a collection
Add POST /api/collections/{collectionId}/items/reorder endpoint in CatalogItemsController
Endpoint accepts a JSON body: { itemIds: int[] } representing the new order
Endpoint updates SortOrder for each item based on array index position (0, 1, 2, ...)
Endpoint requires authentication and verifies collection ownership
When creating a new catalog item, auto-assign SortOrder as max + 1 for that collection
Add unit test for the reorder endpoint in CatalogItemEndpointsTests
dotnet build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add SortOrder column to WishlistItems and reorder API endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SortOrder field on WishlistItems and a reorder endpoint so the frontend can persist drag-and-drop order.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SortOrder (int, default 0) property to WishlistItem entity in src/api/GeekVault.Api/Entities/Vault/WishlistItem.cs
Generate and apply EF Core migration: dotnet ef migrations add AddWishlistItemSortOrder
Update WishlistRepository to order by SortOrder by default when returning wishlist items
Add POST /api/collections/{collectionId}/wishlist/reorder endpoint in WishlistController
Endpoint accepts a JSON body: { itemIds: int[] } representing the new order
Endpoint updates SortOrder for each wishlist item based on array index position (0, 1, 2, ...)
Endpoint requires authentication and verifies collection ownership
When creating a new wishlist item, auto-assign SortOrder as max + 1 for that collection
Add unit test for the reorder endpoint in WishlistEndpointsTests
dotnet build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add drag-and-drop to custom field builder and wishlist
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag-and-drop to reorder custom fields in collection types and wishlist items.
acceptanceCriteria: Collection type custom field builder uses SortableList component for reordering fields
Drag handle (GripVertical icon) on each field row is the drag trigger
Reordered fields persist when dialog is saved
Wishlist items within a collection group can be reordered via drag-and-drop
After wishlist reorder, call POST /api/collections/{id}/wishlist/reorder with new item ID order
Visual feedback during drag: shadow + slight scale on dragged item

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add drag-and-drop reordering for collection items in grid view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag-and-drop items in the collection grid view to reorder them, with the order persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid view items in collection detail are wrapped in SortableList component
User can drag items to reorder them within the grid
After drop, call POST /api/collections/{id}/items/reorder with the new item ID order
Optimistic UI update: items reorder immediately, revert if API call fails
Visual feedback during drag: shadow + scale on dragged card

userStories.title: Responsive polish for mobile breakpoints
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want all pages to look good and be usable on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages tested at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px) breakpoints
Dialogs render full-width on mobile (no side margins, max-w-full on sm:)
Touch targets are minimum 44x44px on all interactive elements
No horizontal overflow on any page at 375px width
Images use loading='lazy' attribute and proper aspect-ratio CSS
Command palette renders full-width on mobile
Cards stack to single column on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Complete i18n audit for all new UI strings
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all new strings translated to keep the app fully bilingual.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all components for hardcoded English strings
Add all missing keys to en.json with English text
